stem: A proposal enters the grant application pipeline. The probability of passing departmental review is 90%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing external peer review is 85%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing panel discussion is between 65% and 84% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing funding decision is 71%. What is the overall probability that the proposal is funded?

ground_truth.answer: P(funded) ranges from 0.353 to 0.456 as the panel discussion pass rate varies from 65% to 84%. Point estimate: 0.402.

solution_steps: The proposal must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the panel discussion stage creates a 10.3 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the panel discussion rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
